title: Generator Test Results
spl:
index=power sourcetype="generator:test"
| stats latest(result) as last_result, latest(_time) as last_test by generator_id
| eval days_since_test=round((now()-last_test)/86400)
| where last_result!="pass" OR days_since_test > 30
description: Generators are the last line of defense during extended outages. Failed tests mean they may not start when needed.
value: Generators are the last line of defense during extended outages. Failed tests mean they may not start when needed.
implementation: Log generator test results (manual or automated). Track test frequency and outcomes. Alert on failed tests and missed test schedules. Monitor fuel levels. Report on generator readiness for management.
visualization: Table (generator test history), Single value (days since last test), Status indicator (pass/fail).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: BMS integration, manual log input.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Generator controller logs, BMS events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Log generator test results (manual or automated). Track test frequency and outcomes. Alert on failed tests and missed test schedules. Monitor fuel levels. Report on generator readiness for management.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="generator:test"
| stats latest(result) as last_result, latest(_time) as last_test by generator_id
| eval days_since_test=round((now()-last_test)/86400)
| where last_result!="pass" OR days_since_test > 30
```

Understanding this SPL

**Generator Test Results** — Generators are the last line of defense during extended outages. Failed tests mean they may not start when needed.

Documented **Data sources**: Generator controller logs, BMS events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): BMS integration, manual log input. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: generator:test. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="generator:test". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by generator_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since_test** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where last_result!="pass" OR days_since_test > 30` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (generator test history), Single value (days since last test), Status indicator (pass/fail).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
knownFalsePositives: Power spikes during equipment power cycling, scheduled load shifting, or DR drills.
